# AgentCamp — Original Page Content

> A 4-week program to build AI agents and deploy to production — no code required. From experimenting with ChatGPT to operating automated workflows.

By [Frutero](https://frutero.club) · 100% Virtual · 50 spots · Cohort 1

---

## Hero

**100% Virtual · 4-week program**

Build AI Agents. Deploy to Production. No Code.

You've spent months playing with ChatGPT and Claude. Copying prompts. Testing tools. But you have nothing in production.

Agentcamp takes you from experimenting to operating in 4 weeks.

Cohort 2 · Only 50 spots

- [Reserve Your Spot](https://tally.so/r/aQ2D0b)
- [View Program](#curriculum)

---

## Pain Points

The problem isn't AI. It's the gap between demo and production.

- **Scattered Prompts**: Every conversation starts from scratch. No system. No memory. No flow.
- **Demos That Die**: It works on your laptop. You show it excitedly. It never makes it to production.
- **Zero Control**: The agent hallucinates. Does random things. And you have no idea how to debug it.

---

## Transformation

In 4 weeks, this changes:

| Before | After |
|--------|-------|
| You experiment with prompts | You operate automated workflows |
| Local demos | Systems deployed to the cloud |
| Agents that hallucinate | Agents with control and oversight |
| "Someday I'll learn" | Certified Agent Operator |

---

## The Method: Orchestration-First

Most courses teach: "build an agent, then try to control it."

We flip the model:

**Control first. Intelligence second.**

You start with workflows and state management. Agent autonomy comes later—within boundaries you define.

This is how production teams actually build.

1. **Orchestration Layer** — Flow control
2. **Agent Capabilities** — Intelligence
3. **Human Oversight** — Supervision
4. **Production** — Real deployment

---

## Curriculum

4 Weeks. 8 Sessions. 4 Real Projects.

### Week 1 — Orchestration Foundations
*"Control before intelligence"*

**What you learn:**
- Event-driven workflow design
- State management for agents
- Your first workflow without LLMs

**What you build:**
Smart Content Processor — text, URLs, documents in → classified, extracted, routed data out.

### Week 2 — From Workflows to Agents
*"Agents live inside workflows"*

**What you learn:**
- Agent anatomy: role, objectives, tools
- Multi-agent collaboration patterns
- The Researcher → Analyst → Writer pattern

**What you build:**
Content Generation Pipeline — 3 agents working as a team → ready-to-publish content.

### Week 3 — Real-World Integration
*"Agents that connect with the real world"*

**What you learn:**
- Integration with email, calendar, messaging
- Human-in-the-loop patterns
- Approval workflows and feedback loops

**What you build:**
Personal Assistant with Human Oversight — inbox triage, drafts with approval → real actions, human supervision.

### Week 4 — Production & Operations
*"From your laptop to the cloud"*

**What you learn:**
- Cloud deployment and architecture
- Monitoring and observability
- Production debugging

**What you build:**
Your Agent in Production — deployed, monitored, documented, with complete operational runbook.

**Don't know how to code? No problem.**
You use AI coding assistants (Cursor, Claude Code) to generate code. You direct. The AI writes. We teach you to orchestrate.

---

Agentcamp runs on the same stack used by Silicon Valley teams.

- Partner Framework
- LLM Provider
- Integration Platform
- Cloud
- Monitoring

Part of the Frutero ecosystem—LATAM's most active builder community.

- +1,000 builders trained
- 32.7% completion rate
- 69% deployment rate

---

## Is Agentcamp for You?

### It's for you if...
- You're a founder, marketer, ops lead, or creator
- You've used ChatGPT/Claude for months but have nothing in production
- You want automated systems, not scattered prompts
- You have 5-7 hours per week to dedicate
- You're ready to build, not just consume content

### It's not for you if...
- You're looking for a theoretical machine learning course
- You expect AI to do everything without your supervision
- You can't commit the weekly time
- You're already a senior developer looking for advanced content
- You want results without doing the work

- Sessions: 8 live
- Cohort: Maximum 50

- 8 live sessions + recordings
- 4 projects with feedback
- Access to Operators community
- Office hours 2x per week
- Async support during the program

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Do I need to know how to code?**
No. We use AI coding assistants (Cursor, Claude Code) to generate code. You learn to direct and orchestrate, not write Python from scratch. Actual requirement: know how to use a computer and be willing to learn new tools.

**How much time do I need to dedicate per week?**
5-7 hours weekly: 3-4 hours in live sessions, 2-3 hours of independent work and projects. The program is designed for people with full-time jobs.

**What tools will I use?**
The stack includes: IDE with AI assistant (Cursor or VS Code + Copilot), Agent framework (defined with sponsors), Claude API for LLM calls, Composio for integrations, Cloud platform for deployment. Everything is set up in pre-work before starting.

**Are the sessions live or recorded?**
Live with interactive Q&A. Recordings are available for review, but the value is in participating live.

**What if I fall behind?**
You have office hours 2x per week to catch up. Async support in the community for specific questions. Access to recordings for review. The program has a pace, but there's a support network.

**What kind of agents can I build after?**
The program gives you the foundation to build: email and calendar assistants, automated content pipelines, research and analysis agents, customer support workflows, internal operational automations. The limit is your use case, not the technical knowledge.

**Is there a community after the program?**
Yes. Permanent access to the Agentcamp community. Networking with other graduates. Content updates.

**Why is it free?**
The program is sponsored by AI infrastructure companies that want more builders in LATAM to learn to build with their technology. You learn for free, they gain adoption. Win-win.

---
